title: Capacity, Observability, Support, and Production Operations · Product Reasoning Brief: Incident Game Day
summary: The capstone is feature-complete but has no realistic workload, user-centered service measures, operator workflow, support path, capacity boundary, or incident rehearsal. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone incident game day increment in operational readiness and capacity case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
Create symptom, impact, evidence, decision, mitigation, escalation, rollback, recovery, communication, and verification runbooks tested by another operator.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Incident Game Day · Theory Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
theory case — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Incident Game Day contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Incident Game Day · Theory Incident Game Day.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure. For Predict Incident Game Day · Theory Incident Game Day, keep Incident Game Day connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. content.text: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Incident Game Day while producing operational readiness and capacity case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure. A defensible solution applies Incident Game Day, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to operational readiness and capacity case.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-incident-game-day — Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
focus: capstone-incident-game-day
decision-evidence: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

steps.title: Defend the Incident Game Day model · Theory Incident Game Day

content.title: Evidence defense
